note: The chamber's own title. 'riksdagsledamot' exists only as this replacement, so the ratio is a real choice between two competing words. Best-attested case in the corpus (96.9% neutral).
Coinage — but barely used (2.5%). The contrast with riksdagsledamot is the finding: the chamber renamed itself, not its civil servants.
Coinage with zero corpus uptake (0.0%). The official agency titles (DO, JO) retain -man, which is likely why.

NOT a ratio: 'lärare' is the unmarked base word for teacher and always covered everyone — it was never coined to replace 'lärarinna'. A ratio here would just measure how often anyone says 'teacher' (19 013 hits) and read as a meaningless 99.9%. What is real is the near-disappearance of the marked feminine form.
Same: 'skådespelare' is the base word, not a coinage. Track the decline of the marked form, not a ratio.
Deliberate null case: ISOF records no settled neutral alternative, so there is nothing to substitute toward. Tracked to show absence of change where none is claimed — not to imply one is owed.
Deliberate null case: the neutral coinage has zero corpus occurrences. Reported as found.
